arts.ttl: “Perhaps our best player” – Big praise from Thomas Frank for Brentford man, up there with Mbuemo
arts.sum: Brentford manager Thomas Frank has heaped praise on Mikkel Damsgaard, believing he’s been as important at Bryan Mbuemo for the club this season. Tipsbladet cover comments from the Brentford manager today made to Viaplay, in which he praises the 24-year-old for his performances and the manner in which he’s stepped up this season. arts.ttl: Chelsea v Morecambe, Leicester v QPR, and more: FA Cup third round – clockwatch
arts.sum: Updates from the 3pm GMT kick-offs Live scoreboard | Get in touch with Tom Ilias Chair is the first QPR player to test Jakub Stolarczyk, who saves well, but the Leicester keeper cannot do anything about Jonathan Varane’s effort. QPR had turned up the heat in the previous five minutes or so and after Harry Winks failed to clear, Varane gathered the ball and slammed it low and into the bottom corner from all of 25 yards. Nottingham Forest: Carlos Miguel, Toffolo, Boly, Morato, Alex, Moreira, Ward-Prowse, Jota Silva, Yates, Sosa, Awoniyi. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Brentford and Leicester both make inquiry for winger – May need to move quickly as transfer rivals step ahead
arts.sum: Brentford and Leicester City have both shown an interest in Troyes youngster Cyriaque Irié and could provide competition to Lens for the 19-year-old. That’s according to Foot Mercato, who report that while the French side have been ‘seduced’ by the youngster, they may have to move ahead of Brentford and Leicester to get him. arts.ttl: Brentford's Aaron Hickey provides injury update after signing new long-term contract
arts.sum: Brentford defender Aaron Hickey provides update on his injury recovery after signing a new long-term contract with the Premier League club.

arts.ttl: Plymouth appoint Austrian Muslic as new boss
arts.sum: Plymouth Argyle appoint Austrian Miron Muslic as their new head coach, succeeding Wayne Rooney.

arts.ttl: Plymouth appoint Austrian coach Miron Muslic to succeed Wayne Rooney
arts.sum: Muslic managed Cercle Brugge until December sacking ‘Miron comes with glowing reviews’ says Argyle chairman Plymouth have appointed Miron Muslic as the club’s new head coach to replace Wayne Rooney. The 42-year-old former Cercle Brugge manager has signed a three-and-a-half year deal to succeed Rooney, who left the club on New Year’s Eve. Continue reading...
